html:
Exclusive to:

The Frostjaw is a Boss exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It is a large humanoid shark with icy features. It can be spawned from the Ice Fishing Hole on the Ice Sheet, which is in the Rough Ocean. To summon Frostjaw, the player must catch 3 Deep Bass with a Sea Fishing Rod at the Ice Fishing Hole. After that, the Ice Fishing Hole will not disappear and the player can keep fishing. However, only one Frostjaw will spawn.

Contents

  • 1 Behavior
    • 1.1 Winter's Feast
  • 2 Fighting Strategy
  • 3 Names
  • 4 Quotes
  • 5 Ice
  • 6 Trivia
  • 7 Assets
  • 8 Gallery
  • 9 Sounds

Behavior

By default, the Frostjaw is passive towards the player and simply walks around. Once attacked, it becomes aggressive and starts fighting.

It has 3 attacks rotating in this order:

  1. It performs a melee attack with two swipes, followed by a third swipe with its tail which knocks targets back.
  2. It summons two walls of Ice Spikes in the direction of the target and dives between the walls, dealing damage and knocking targets back. Depending on the dive's angle, it may destroy only some of the Ice Spikes, leaving the rest as obstacles.
  3. It swims under the ice with its fin visible on the surface, and torpedoes out of it to hit its target, knocking them back and drilling back into the ground. The Frostjaw deals damage and knockback on exit as well as where it lands.

When fighting on a bigger island (in Winter), the Frostjaw's dive attack causes the island to slowly fall apart at the borders, creating Ice Boats that float away and sink after 30 seconds. This diminishes the size of the fighting area, until it reaches the minimum size of a two-tile radius around the center of the island where the Ice Fishing Hole was.

On defeat, rather than being killed, the Frostjaw gives up and becomes passive again, and can no longer be attacked. The Frostjaw despawns once the area is unloaded, along with the entire Ice Sheet.

After being defeated, the player can trade up to five Ocean Fishes with the Frostjaw for one Bootleg Getaway per fish traded. The player can only trade Ocean Fishes with 1 Fish Value (e.g., Deep Bass) and Corn Cod. Each heavy Ocean Fish will grant two Bootleg Getaways instead of one. The first heavy fish trade will also give a Sketch for Frostjaw Figure.

Winter's Feast

Only during the Event: Winter's Feast
During Winter's Feast, the first heavy fish trade with Frostjaw will give one large Gift instead of a Sketch for Frostjaw Figure. The Gift contains a Frostjaw Magnificent Adornment, a Sketch for Frostjaw Figure and 2 Winter Food.

Fighting Strategy

The Frostjaw's three-hit combo attack can be avoided by circling around it. Since the attack is directional and can't hit anything at the boss' sides, the attack will get cancelled early and it won't be able to perform its tail attack. Hit it five times if it did one swipe, or six times if it did two swipes. After the boss attempts its three-hit combo attack a few times, it will attempt to trap the player with walls of ice to perform its ice-slide attack, or dive below the ice to perform the dive-bomb attack.

Moving quickly away to the sides can allow the player to avoid being trapped between the walls of ice once they form. If the player doesn't escape from the path of the attack before the ice walls form, a strong pickaxe tool, such as a Pick/Axe or Brightshade Smasher should be used to break the wall to escape (A normal Pickaxe can break the ice spikes as well, but require two hits). Once the Frostjaw reaches the end of its slide, it will sit stunned, allowing the player to get many more hits in. Short-range teleportation such as the Lazy Explorer or Wanda's Backstep Watch can also be used to dodge the attack if the player is trapped.

If the Frostjaw performs its dive bomb attack, make as much distance as possible between yourself and where its fin disappeared below the ice, giving a better chance to avoid damage compared to running to the sides. When the Frostjaw lands on the ground after leaping at the player, its head will be stuck in the ground for a short moment before it repeats the attack. If the player deals any kind of damage while the Frostjaw is stuck, it will pull itself out and be stunned for several seconds, giving the player a chance to get many hits in and ending that attack pattern early. The Frostjaw will signal that its stun duration is about to end by standing up and shaking its head. Its attack patterns then repeat.

Good weaponry (Such as a Ham Bat or at least two Dark Swords worth of durability) and decent armor (such as Football Helmets and Log Suits) will help with the fight substantially as the fight can be somewhat unfair to the player due to the slippery ice terrain, causing the player to slip and fall, stunning them and allowing the boss to land hits on the player. Speed-boosting items (such as a Walking Cane) are recommended to make avoiding attacks easier. However, the more speed buff the player has, the higher chance they will have to slip and fall while running. Once the player begins to slip, they can stop moving immediately to avoid falling over.

If the fight goes into night time, bringing Lanterns or other forms of light are a wise decision as the Frostjaw can destroy campfires with its attacks. If lanterns are used, they may also be flung away by the Frostjaw's attacks, so taking backups is recommended.

Below is the number of hits it takes with each weapon to kill a Frostjaw when playing with characters with a default damage modifier. The Weather Pain, Brightshade Staff, and Bee Mine are not included due to their random natures. This table does not take into account the damage bonus gained from characters with a lunar or shadow affinity. It also does not take into account the 40% damage bonus Wendy gets from attacking the same mob as Abigail, Wanda's damage bonus with Shadow weapons (except for the Alarming Clock, which does accurately reflect that bonus), or Wolfgang's damage multiplier when he isn't mighty.

It takes 4 blasts with an Ice Staff to freeze Frostjaw, and 4 Sleep Darts to put it to sleep.

Quotes

When Idling

  • "HUH? WHAT HAPPENED?"
  • "HEY! WHATSA MATTER WITH YOU?"
  • "I FEEL LIKE A FISH OUTTA WATER..."
  • "WHADAYA WANT, CHOWDERHEAD?"
  • "I WAS JUST LOOKIN' FOR A QUICK BITE."
  • "WHADAYA LOOKIN' AT?"
  • "YOU WANT A PIECE OF ME, TOUGH GUY?"
  • "YOU CRUISIN' FOR A BRUISIN?"
  • "WHY I OUGHTA..."

While fighting

  • "TOUGH GUY, EH?"
  • "YOU'LL NEVER CATCH ME, SEE."
  • "YOUSE DON'T STAND A CHANCE."
  • "IT'S BITIN' TIME!"
  • "YOU'RE A REAL PIECE OF WORK, BUDDY."
  • "YOU DON'T GET NO PASS."
  • "I'LL PUT YOUSE ON ICE!"
  • "FREEZE!"
  • "YOUSE ON THIN ICE NOW!"
  • "YOU'LL BE SLEEPIN' WITH THE FISHES!"
  • "I'M NOT GONNA LET THIS SLIDE!"
  • "ICE TO EAT YA! HEH HEH."

Cheering

  • "PUT 'EM ON ICE!"
  • "WHACK 'EM!"
  • "GIVE 'EM THE OL' ONE-TWO!"
  • "FROSTBITE!"
  • "CHOMP! CHOMP!"
  • "CLIP 'EM!"
  • "ICE 'EM!"
  • "THEY AIN'T NO FRIEND OF OURS!"
  • "THIS IS OUR TURF!"
  • "FIN-ISH 'EM!"

Gloating

  • "YOUSE BEEN ICED."
  • "SEE YOUSE IN THE NEXT LIFE."
  • "FUHGEDDABOUDIT."
  • "END OF THE LINE."
  • "YOU GOT CAUGHT."
  • "YOU'RE FIN-ISHED."
  • "BELLY-UP."
  • "GAVE 'EM A BURIAL AT SEA."
  • "HOOK, LINE AND SINKER."
  • "VICTORY TASTES GOOD."
  • "GUTTED."
  • "SLEEPIN' WITH THE FISHES."
  • "YOU'RE DONE, CHUM."
  • "WHERE YOU BELONG, BOTTOM-FEEDER."
  • "I GOT BIGGER FISH TO FRY."
  • "LOOKIN' A LIL' GREEN AROUND THE GILLS THERE, MAC."

Giving up once defeated

  • "I BIT OFF MORE THAN I CAN CHEW."
  • "I WAS OUTTA MY DEPTH."
  • "UNCLE! UNCLE!"
  • "CAN'T WE WORK SOMETHIN' OUT?"
  • "YOU GOT ME BY THE GILLS."
  • "I'M FIN-ISHED."
  • "I FEEL S- SO COLD..."
  • "COLD-BLOODED, ACE. COLD-BLOODED."
  • "I'M A FISH OUTTA WATER."
  • "SPARE ME. I DIDN'T KNOW YOUSE WAS SO STRONG."
  • "I GIVE UP, I GIVE UP. YEESH."
  • "HOW DO YA FEEL ABOUT CATCH AND RELEASE?"
  • "ALRIGHT IT'S YOUR TURF NOW, BOSS."
  • "OKAY, YOU CAUGHT ME FAIR AND SQUARE."
  • "HOW'S ABOUT A TRUCE?"
  • "I CAN'T GO BACK INSIDE, LEMME WORK FOR YOU, BOSS."
  • "MERCY, MERCY! YOU GOT ME."
  • "OWW. YOU GOT WATER IN MY EYE. THAT REALLY HURT."
  • "I SURRENDER, QUIT WHALE'N ON ME."

When Idling (After defeat)

  • "WOAH, WOAH! NO HARD FEELINGS, ALRIGHT?"
  • "I CAN MAKE YOUSE AN OFFER... IF YOUSE GOT THE GOODS."
  • "LET'S COME TO AN ARRANGEMENT, IF YOU CATCH MY DRIFT."
  • "LISTEN, MAYBE YOUSE AND ME AIN'T SO DIFFERENT."
  • "I THINK WE COULD DO SOME BUSINESS, YOU AND ME."
  • "HOWSABOUT YOU SCRATCH MY FIN AND I SCRATCH YOURS?"
  • "WHADAYA SAY TO A LIL' SQUID PRO QUO?"
  • "I AIN'T ONE TO REFUSE A NICE JUICY FISH. BIGGER THE BETTER."
  • "DON'T WANT NO STINKIN' POND SCUM."
  • "I'M ON A SEA FOOD DIET. I ONLY EATS IT IF IT'S FROM THE SEA."

Receiving an item

  • "HOLY MACKEREL, THAT'S A BIG ONE."
  • "NOW, THAT IS A BEAUTIFUL FISH."
  • "AIN'T GOT NO MORE. I'M CLEANED OUT."
  • "IF IT AIN'T A FISH FROM THE OCEAN, I DON'T WANT IT."
  • "DON'T INSULT ME WITH THIS PUNY THING. GIMME A BIG ONE."

Attempt Trade

  • "INTERESTIN', REEL INTERESTIN'."
  • "WHAT WE GOT HERE?"
  • "LET'S SEA WHATCHA GOT."
  • "WHATCHA FISHIN' FOR?"
  • "HOPE YOUSE AIN'T WASTIN' MY TIME."
  • "BETTER NOT BE NO RAW DEAL."
  • "I DON'T WANT NO BAIT N' SWITCH."
  • "OKAY, YOU REELED ME IN. WHATCHA GOT?"

Following the player

  • "SURE, SKIP!"
  • "OK, BOSS!"
  • "AY CAP'N!"
  • "TIL MY LAST BREATH!"
  • "EASY PEASY."
  • "WHATCHA NEED, BOSS?"
  • "YOU'RE A FRIEND OF OURS."
  • "YOU GOT IT, BOSS."
  • "WHY SO GLUM, CHUM?"
  • "LEMME AT 'EM."
  • "I'D NEVER EAT YOU, BOSS."
  • "JUST SAY THE WORD, BOSS."
  • "I GOT YOUR BACK."
  • "YOU SAY SOMETHIN', BOSS?"
  • "BOSS IS THE BEST."
  • "WHO WE WHACKIN' BOSS?"
  • "LOOKIN' SHARP, BOSS."
  • "WHERE TO, SKIP?"

Ice

The Ice Spike or Ice is an object exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It is spawned by the Frostjaw during its dive attack, and remains indefinitely until it melts or gets destroyed. It can be mined with a Pickaxe, dropping 1 Ice with a 75% chance for a second Ice. If it is destroyed by the Frostjaw, it does not drop Ice.

Ice spikes can be mined in one hit when using Pick/Axe or Brightshade Smasher, or while under the effects of Honey Crystals.

Trivia

  • The Frostjaw was introduced in the Skill Spotlight: Wigfrid & Willow update.
  • In beta after defeat, it dropped 2 Bootleg Getaways with a 50% chance for a third one.
  • The prefab name "sharkboi" may be a reference to the movie "Sharkboy and Lavagirl"
  • Frostjaws as a species may be related to Rockjaws, but adapted for colder climates. Due to a lack of Lunar Alignment it is unlikely that they are a lunar mutation despite other creatures gaining similar icy powers upon mutating.

The Frostjaw is a Boss exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It is a large humanoid shark with icy features. It can be spawned from the Ice Fishing Hole on the Ice Sheet, which is in the Rough Ocean.
To summon Frostjaw, the player must catch 3 Deep Bass with a Sea Fishing Rod at the Ice Fishing Hole. After that, the Ice Fishing Hole will not disappear and the player can keep fishing. However, only one Frostjaw will spawn.

Behavior[edit | edit source]
By default, the Frostjaw is passive towards the player and simply walks around. Once attacked, it becomes aggressive and starts fighting.
It has 3 attacks rotating in this order:
It performs a melee attack with two swipes, followed by a third swipe with its tail which knocks targets back.
It summons two walls of Ice Spikes in the direction of the target and dives between the walls, dealing damage and knocking targets back. Depending on the dive's angle, it may destroy only some of the Ice Spikes, leaving the rest as obstacles.
It swims under the ice with its fin visible on the surface, and torpedoes out of it to hit its target, knocking them back and drilling back into the ground. The Frostjaw deals damage and knockback on exit as well as where it lands.
When fighting on a bigger island (in Winter), the Frostjaw's dive attack causes the island to slowly fall apart at the borders, creating Ice Boats that float away and sink after 30 seconds. This diminishes the size of the fighting area, until it reaches the minimum size of a two-tile radius around the center of the island where the Ice Fishing Hole was.
On defeat, rather than being killed, the Frostjaw gives up and becomes passive again, and can no longer be attacked. The Frostjaw despawns once the area is unloaded, along with the entire Ice Sheet.
After being defeated, the player can trade up to five Ocean Fishes with the Frostjaw for one Bootleg Getaway per fish traded. The player can only trade Ocean Fishes with 1 Fish Value (e.g., Deep Bass) and Corn Cod. Each heavy Ocean Fish will grant two Bootleg Getaways instead of one. The first heavy fish trade will also give a Sketch for Frostjaw Figure.
Winter's Feast[edit | edit source]
Only during the Event: Winter's Feast
During Winter's Feast, the first heavy fish trade with Frostjaw will give one large Gift instead of a Sketch for Frostjaw Figure. The Gift contains a Frostjaw Magnificent Adornment, a Sketch for Frostjaw Figure and 2 Winter Food.
Fighting Strategy[edit | edit source]
The Frostjaw's three-hit combo attack can be avoided by circling around it. Since the attack is directional and can't hit anything at the boss' sides, the attack will get cancelled early and it won't be able to perform its tail attack. Hit it five times if it did one swipe, or six times if it did two swipes. After the boss attempts its three-hit combo attack a few times, it will attempt to trap the player with walls of ice to perform its ice-slide attack, or dive below the ice to perform the dive-bomb attack.
Moving quickly away to the sides can allow the player to avoid being trapped between the walls of ice once they form. If the player doesn't escape from the path of the attack before the ice walls form, a strong pickaxe tool, such as a Pick/Axe or Brightshade Smasher should be used to break the wall to escape (A normal Pickaxe can break the ice spikes as well, but require two hits). Once the Frostjaw reaches the end of its slide, it will sit stunned, allowing the player to get many more hits in. Short-range teleportation such as the Lazy Explorer or Wanda's Backstep Watch can also be used to dodge the attack if the player is trapped.
If the Frostjaw performs its dive bomb attack, make as much distance as possible between yourself and where its fin disappeared below the ice, giving a better chance to avoid damage compared to running to the sides. When the Frostjaw lands on the ground after leaping at the player, its head will be stuck in the ground for a short moment before it repeats the attack. If the player deals any kind of damage while the Frostjaw is stuck, it will pull itself out and be stunned for several seconds, giving the player a chance to get many hits in and ending that attack pattern early. The Frostjaw will signal that its stun duration is about to end by standing up and shaking its head. Its attack patterns then repeat.
Good weaponry (Such as a Ham Bat or at least two Dark Swords worth of durability) and decent armor (such as Football Helmets and Log Suits) will help with the fight substantially as the fight can be somewhat unfair to the player due to the slippery ice terrain, causing the player to slip and fall, stunning them and allowing the boss to land hits on the player. Speed-boosting items (such as a Walking Cane) are recommended to make avoiding attacks easier. However, the more speed buff the player has, the higher chance they will have to slip and fall while running. Once the player begins to slip, they can stop moving immediately to avoid falling over.
If the fight goes into night time, bringing Lanterns or other forms of light are a wise decision as the Frostjaw can destroy campfires with its attacks. If lanterns are used, they may also be flung away by the Frostjaw's attacks, so taking backups is recommended.
Below is the number of hits it takes with each weapon to kill a Frostjaw when playing with characters with a default damage modifier. The Weather Pain, Brightshade Staff, and Bee Mine are not included due to their random natures. This table does not take into account the damage bonus gained from characters with a lunar or shadow affinity. It also does not take into account the 40% damage bonus Wendy gets from attacking the same mob as Abigail, Wanda's damage bonus with Shadow weapons (except for the Alarming Clock, which does accurately reflect that bonus), or Wolfgang's damage multiplier when he isn't mighty.

×1-2
Miscellaneous
"sharkboi_icespike"
The Ice Spike or Ice is an object exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It is spawned by the Frostjaw during its dive attack, and remains indefinitely until it melts or gets destroyed. It can be mined with a Pickaxe, dropping 1 Ice with a 75% chance for a second Ice. If it is destroyed by the Frostjaw, it does not drop Ice.
Ice spikes can be mined in one hit when using Pick/Axe or Brightshade Smasher, or while under the effects of Honey Crystals.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Frostjaw was introduced in the Skill Spotlight: Wigfrid & Willow update.
In beta after defeat, it dropped 2 Bootleg Getaways with a 50% chance for a third one.
The prefab name "sharkboi" may be a reference to the movie "Sharkboy and Lavagirl"
Frostjaws as a species may be related to Rockjaws, but adapted for colder climates. Due to a lack of Lunar Alignment it is unlikely that they are a lunar mutation despite other creatures gaining similar icy powers upon mutating.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Frostjaw là Boss độc quyền của Don't Starve Together. Nó là một con cá mập hình người lớn với những đặc điểm băng giá. Nó có thể được sinh ra từ Lỗ Câu Cá Trên Băng trên Dải Băng, ở Biển Động. Để triệu hồi Frostjaw, người chơi phải bắt được 3 con Deep Bass bằng Cần câu biển tại Lỗ câu cá trên băng. Sau đó, Lỗ câu cá trên băng sẽ không biến mất và người chơi có thể tiếp tục câu cá. Tuy nhiên, chỉ có một Frostjaw sẽ xuất hiện.
title: Frostjaw
wikitext:
{{Object Infobox |image = Frostjaw.png |health = 8000 |damage = {{Damagereceived|37.5| to player}} 75 to mobs |walkSpeed = 2.3 |attackRange = 8
4.5 (melee) |runSpeed = 8 (6 while burrowed) |attackPeriod = 3 |drops = |spawnCode = "sharkboi" |spawnFrom = {{pic|32|Ice Fishing Hole}} (when fishing with {{pic|32|Sea Fishing Rod}}) }} {{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{Quotes |maxwell = Who dreamed up this icy abomination? |walter = Better watch out for frostbite! |wanda = A brute if I've ever seen one! |warly = It looks rather tough. |wigfrid = Whether land ör sea, a shallöw grave waits för thee. |webber = Maybe it just wants to play with us. |wendy = It has a bite no colder than reality. |wickerbottom = This brute has broken innumerable laws of nature, and I simply will not have it. |willow = Fire beats ice any day of the week. |wilson = Shiver me timbers! |winona = You picked the wrong gal to mess with! |wolfgang = Wolfgang is stronger than any fish! |woodie = I normally like ice fishin', but not when the fish bite back! |wormwood = Icy Chomper! |wortox = That's one cool customer, hyuyu! |wurt = Grrr, bad fishie! |wx78 = IT HIDES ITS FLESHBODY UNDER COLD, INORGANIC MATERIAL. COMMENDABLE |choose = woodie }} The '''Frostjaw''' is a Boss exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. It is a large humanoid shark with icy features. It can be spawned from the [[Ice Fishing Hole]] on the [[Ice Sheet]], which is in the [[Ocean#Rough|Rough Ocean]]. To summon Frostjaw, the player must catch 3 [[Deep Bass]] with a [[Sea Fishing Rod]] at the Ice Fishing Hole. After that, the Ice Fishing Hole will not disappear and the player can keep fishing. However, only one Frostjaw will spawn. =={{Pic32|Brain}} Behavior== By default, the Frostjaw is passive towards the player and simply walks around. Once attacked, it becomes aggressive and starts fighting. It has 3 attacks rotating in this order: # It performs a melee attack with two swipes, followed by a third swipe with its tail which knocks targets back. # It summons two walls of [[Ice Spike]]s in the direction of the target and dives between the walls, dealing damage and knocking targets back. Depending on the dive's angle, it may destroy only some of the Ice Spikes, leaving the rest as obstacles. # It swims under the ice with its fin visible on the surface, and torpedoes out of it to hit its target, knocking them back and drilling back into the ground. The Frostjaw deals damage and knockback on exit as well as where it lands. When fighting on a bigger island (in Winter), the Frostjaw's dive attack causes the island to slowly fall apart at the borders, creating [[Ice Boat]]s that float away and sink after 30 seconds. This diminishes the size of the fighting area, until it reaches the minimum size of a two-tile radius around the center of the island where the Ice Fishing Hole was. On defeat, rather than being killed, the Frostjaw gives up and becomes passive again, and can no longer be attacked. The Frostjaw despawns once the area is unloaded, along with the entire [[Ice Sheet]]. After being defeated, the player can trade up to five [[Ocean Fishes]] with the Frostjaw for one [[Bootleg Getaway]] per fish traded. The player can only trade Ocean Fishes with 1 [[Food Value/DST#Fish/Seafood values|Fish Value]] (e.g., Deep Bass) and [[Corn Cod]]. Each heavy Ocean Fish will grant two Bootleg Getaways instead of one. The first heavy fish trade will also give a [[Sketch]] for [[Frostjaw Figure]]. ===Winter's Feast=== {{Event|Winter's Feast}} During Winter's Feast, the first heavy fish trade with Frostjaw will give one large [[Gift]] instead of a Sketch for Frostjaw Figure. The Gift contains a Frostjaw [[Magnificent Adornment]], a Sketch for Frostjaw Figure and 2 [[Winter Food]]. =={{Pic32|Spear}} Fighting Strategy== The Frostjaw's three-hit combo attack can be avoided by circling around it. Since the attack is directional and can't hit anything at the boss' sides, the attack will get cancelled early and it won't be able to perform its tail attack. Hit it five times if it did one swipe, or six times if it did two swipes. After the boss attempts its three-hit combo attack a few times, it will attempt to trap the player with walls of ice to perform its ice-slide attack, or dive below the ice to perform the dive-bomb attack. Moving quickly away to the sides can allow the player to avoid being trapped between the walls of ice once they form. If the player doesn't escape from the path of the attack before the ice walls form, a strong pickaxe tool, such as a [[Pick/Axe]] or [[Brightshade Smasher]] should be used to break the wall to escape (A normal [[Pickaxe]] can break the ice spikes as well, but require two hits). Once the Frostjaw reaches the end of its slide, it will sit stunned, allowing the player to get many more hits in. Short-range teleportation such as the [[The Lazy Explorer/DST|Lazy Explorer]] or [[Wanda]]'s [[Backstep Watch]] can also be used to dodge the attack if the player is trapped. If the Frostjaw performs its dive bomb attack, make as much distance as possible between yourself and where its fin disappeared below the ice, giving a better chance to avoid damage compared to running to the sides. When the Frostjaw lands on the ground after leaping at the player, its head will be stuck in the ground for a short moment before it repeats the attack. If the player deals any kind of damage while the Frostjaw is stuck, it will pull itself out and be stunned for several seconds, giving the player a chance to get many hits in and ending that attack pattern early. The Frostjaw will signal that its stun duration is about to end by standing up and shaking its head. Its attack patterns then repeat. Good weaponry (Such as a [[Ham Bat]] or at least two [[Dark Sword]]s worth of durability) and decent armor (such as [[Football Helmet]]s and [[Log Suit]]s) will help with the fight substantially as the fight can be somewhat unfair to the player due to the slippery ice terrain, causing the player to slip and fall, stunning them and allowing the boss to land hits on the player. Speed-boosting items (such as a [[Walking Cane]]) are recommended to make avoiding attacks easier. However, the more speed buff the player has, the higher chance they will have to slip and fall while running. Once the player begins to slip, they can stop moving immediately to avoid falling over. If the fight goes into night time, bringing [[Lantern]]s or other forms of light are a wise decision as the Frostjaw can destroy campfires with its attacks. If lanterns are used, they may also be flung away by the Frostjaw's attacks, so taking backups is recommended. {{Weapon Hits DST|health=7999|icestaff=4|sleepdart=4}} =={{Pic32|Sign}} Names== Names are randomly assigned. I'M CLEANED OUT." * "IF IT AIN'T A FISH FROM THE OCEAN, I DON'T WANT IT." * "DON'T INSULT ME WITH THIS PUNY THING. GIMME A BIG ONE." '''Attempt Trade''' * "INTERESTIN', REEL INTERESTIN'." * "WHAT WE GOT HERE?" * "LET'S SEA WHATCHA GOT." * "WHATCHA FISHIN' FOR?" * "HOPE YOUSE AIN'T WASTIN' MY TIME." * "BETTER NOT BE NO RAW DEAL." * "I DON'T WANT NO BAIT N' SWITCH." * "OKAY, YOU REELED ME IN. WHATCHA GOT?" '''Following the player''' * "SURE, SKIP!" * "OK, BOSS!" * "AY CAP'N!" * "TIL MY LAST BREATH!" * "EASY PEASY." * "WHATCHA NEED, BOSS?" * "YOU'RE A FRIEND OF OURS." * "YOU GOT IT, BOSS." * "WHY SO GLUM, CHUM?" * "LEMME AT 'EM." * "I'D NEVER EAT YOU, BOSS." * "JUST SAY THE WORD, BOSS." * "I GOT YOUR BACK." * "YOU SAY SOMETHIN', BOSS?" * "BOSS IS THE BEST." * "WHO WE WHACKIN' BOSS?" * "LOOKIN' SHARP, BOSS." * "WHERE TO, SKIP?" {{Col-end}} ==Ice== {{Object Infobox |name = Ice |image = Ice Spike 1.png|1 Ice Spike 2.png|2 Ice Spike 3.png|3 Ice Spike 4.png|4 |spawnFrom = {{pic|32|Frostjaw}} |tool = Can be [[Tools#Mining Tools|Mined]] {{pic|32|Pickaxe}} |resources = {{pic|32|Ice}}×1-2 |spawnCode = "sharkboi_icespike" }} The '''Ice Spike''' or '''Ice''' is an object exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. It is spawned by the [[Frostjaw]] during its dive attack, and remains indefinitely until it melts or gets destroyed. It can be mined with a [[Pickaxe]], dropping 1 Ice with a 75% chance for a second Ice. If it is destroyed by the Frostjaw, it does not drop Ice. Ice spikes can be mined in one hit when using [[Pick/Axe]] or [[Brightshade Smasher]], or while under the effects of [[Honey Crystals]]. {{clear}} == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Frostjaw was introduced in the ''[[Skill Spotlight Update#Wigfrid & Willow|Skill Spotlight: Wigfrid & Willow]]'' update. * In beta after defeat, it dropped 2 [[Bootleg Getaway]]s with a 50% chance for a third one. * The prefab name "sharkboi" may be a reference to the movie "Sharkboy and Lavagirl" * Frostjaws as a species may be related to [[Rockjaw]]s, but adapted for colder climates. Due to a lack of Lunar Alignment it is unlikely that they are a lunar mutation despite [[Possessed Varg|other creatures]] gaining similar icy powers upon mutating. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Frostjaw Spawns.gif|Spawning Frostjaw Walk Up.gif|Walking up Frostjaw Walk Down.gif|Walking down Frostjaw Walk Side.gif|Walking to the side Frostjaw Taunt.gif|Taunting Frostjaw Attack.gif|Attacking Frostjaw Move Under Ice.gif|Moving Under Ice Frostjaw Defeated.gif|Defeated =={{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Frostjaw Old Versions.png|Old Versions Frostjaw Old Textures.png|Old Textures Frostjaw Chat icon.png|Chat icon for NPC Frostjaw sculptures.jpg|All of Frostjaw's craftable sculptures.
